message:
fix(vdbe,persist): correct autoindex B-tree type inference for multi-table schemas (#55)

Autoindex root pages (sqlite_autoindex_*) were misidentified as table
B-trees when MemDatabase::get_table() returned Some for them — because
create_table_at is called for both table and autoindex roots. In schemas
with AUTOINCREMENT + composite UNIQUE constraints, this caused the
autoindex page to be initialized with LeafTable (0x0D) instead of
LeafIndex (0x0A), producing "wrong # of entries in index" and "page N:
never used" errors under stock SQLite integrity_check.

Root cause: zero-page B-tree type inference in VdbeEngine relied solely
on MemDatabase presence, which cannot distinguish autoindex roots from
table roots. Fix: check index_desc_flags_by_root_page and
index_collations_by_root_page first (populated from schema metadata),
and only fall back to MemDatabase when the index maps have no entry.

Also removed the autoindex skip filter in compat_persist so autoindex
B-tree data is written to the persisted file, preventing missing-page
errors.

Closes #55

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
